common.dialogIconPurposeWarning: Dialog type: warning — please review the risk

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Appearance, language, and app info.
settings.languageHint: UI language; saved on this device.
settings.logsFolderWebDevMessage:
If this page is served by the Vite dev server on your machine (`pnpm dev` / `pnpm dev:web`), "Open log folder" asks that dev server to reveal the same directory as the desktop app (Tauri app_log_dir) in your file manager.

If you are viewing a static build, or the dev server runs on another machine, a normal browser still cannot open a folder on your PC. Desktop log location for reference:
{path}

On Linux, if XDG_DATA_HOME is set, use $XDG_DATA_HOME/com.tyutool.desktop/logs instead of ~/.local/share/….

While developing: use the browser DevTools Console for UI logs, and the terminal running tyutool-cli serve for CLI logs.
settings.logsFolderDevOpenFailed: The dev server could not open the log folder: {detail}

update.toastNew: New version v{version} available. Go to Settings to update.
update.portableHint: Portable mode detected. Auto-update is not available for portable installations.
update.toastPortable: New version v{version} available. Please download manually for portable mode.

flash.introLead: Connect the serial port and choose the chip platform
flash.introRest: , then pick Flash / Erase / Authorize; authorization needs UUID and AuthKey.

deviceResetHints.t5: DTR/RTS download-mode pulse (longer hold)—same timing as the link handshake when connecting. Free the serial port first.
deviceResetHints.t1: Same as T5: DTR/RTS download-mode pulse (longer hold)—same timing as the link handshake when connecting. Free the serial port first.
deviceResetHints.t3: DTR/RTS pulse with longer hold—same handshake family when connecting. Free the serial port first.
deviceResetHints.t2: DTR/RTS shorter pulse—same as the handshake when connecting. Free the serial port first.
deviceResetHints.bk7231n: DTR/RTS shorter pulse matching the connect handshake. Free the serial port first.
deviceResetHints.esp32: USB–UART hard-reset sequence (same as after a flash completes; line mapping depends on the board). Free the serial port first.
deviceResetHints.esp32c3: Reset follows the detected USB serial/JTAG path—same as post-flash reset. Free the serial port first.
deviceResetHints.esp32c6: Hard reset matches post-flash reset; interface type is auto-detected. Free the serial port first.
deviceResetHints.esp32s3: Some boards use a USB-JTAG/serial bridge; reset timing matches post-flash reset. Free the serial port first.

flash.deviceOpsHint: Chip platform is selected above; fill only task-specific options here.

tuyaPortHint.maybeFlashAuth: May be the flash / authorize serial port
tuyaPortHint.maybeLog: May be the log serial port
tuyaPortHint.interfaceUnknown: Dual USB serial (1a86:55d2); this OS did not report the USB interface. On Windows, MI_00 is often flash/authorize and MI_02 log; on Linux/macOS check your hardware docs or try each port.

flash.fwHint: Use “Browse” to select a firmware file.
flash.eraseAdvancedHint: Applies a preset erase range for the selected chip platform into the fields above; ranges differ by chip—verify against your hardware docs.
flash.readFileHint: Use 'Browse' to specify where to save the read data.
flash.readDirPlaceholder: Click 'Browse' to select a save directory
flash.readDirHint: Use 'Browse' to select the directory where read data will be saved.
flash.readFileNameHint: Default file name is generated from the chip; you can edit it freely.
flash.authTuyaOpenOnly: TuyaOpen authorization only: use your {link}. Do not substitute keys from TuyaOS products.

err.selectReadFile: Please specify a save file path.
err.selectReadFileLog: Error: no save file path specified.
err.selectReadDir: Please select a save directory first.
err.fillAuthLog: Error: UUID and AuthKey are required for authorization.
err.authorizeOfficialOnly: tyutool does not write authorization over the BootROM flash protocol. Follow the TuyaOpen guide: use auth / auth-read in tos.py monitor, or edit tuya_config.h and rebuild. See the “Device authorization” page on tuyaopen.ai.
err.addrInvalid: Start and end addresses must be valid hexadecimal (optional 0x prefix).
err.startAfterEnd: Start address cannot be greater than end address.
err.verifyFailed: Verification failed: check connection, chip, and firmware match.
err.checksumLog: Error: checksum does not match device report.
err.portUnavailable: Port unavailable. Check connection or release port.

confirm.eraseBody:
About to erase chip {chip}, range {start} – {end}.

This is irreversible. All data in this range will be permanently destroyed.

Continue?
confirm.eraseBodyMisaligned4k:
Chip {chip}: range {start} – {end} must be aligned to {sectorHex} (4 KiB).

Will erase: {alignedStart} – {alignedEnd} (end exclusive).

This cannot be undone. Continue?
confirm.readFileExistsBody:
The file "{path}" already exists.

Overwrite or add a timestamp suffix?

log.portsListFailed: Could not enumerate ports. Check system permissions.
log.browserReadNoDir: Browser mode: the read file will be downloaded automatically; no save directory needed.
log.noPortsFound: No serial ports found. Check device connection and click Refresh.
log.noPortsFoundWebServeHint: Browser preview lists ports from local tyutool-cli serve (default ws://127.0.0.1:9527). On macOS, only /dev/cu.* USB serial devices are shown.
log.deviceResetServeOutdated: Device reset failed: the browser is still talking to an old tyutool-cli serve (no device_reset). Often the new serve failed to bind because port 9527 was busy. Free the port and run pnpm dev:web again (e.g. fuser -k 9527/tcp on Linux).
log.erasePrep: Preparing to erase the selected range…

auth.alreadySame: Device authorization already matches the values to write; no write needed.
auth.noData: No authorization data found on device.

log.authOverwritePrompt: The device already has authorization. Confirm in the dialog whether to overwrite.
log.authProbeSkipped: Could not read device authorization before write ({msg}); proceeding with write.
log.portBusyKillHint: Release command: {cmd} (verify this won't affect other running tasks)

esp.readDeviceInfoFailed: Warning: could not read device info ({error})
esp.eraseAllFlash: No erase range specified — erasing entire flash…
esp.authorizeNotSupported: Authorize mode is not supported for ESP32 chips.

beken.authorizeNotSupported: Authorize mode not supported for this chip.
